title: BUD Switch (Block Update Detector)
description: Detects block updates (block placed, broken, or state changed) adjacent to a quasi-powered piston. The piston is powered by redstone through a diagonal path, putting it in a quasi-powered state — any block update in its update zone triggers it to extend momentarily. Used in advanced farms and hidden triggers.

steps.title: Foundation
steps.explanation: 3-wide, 2-deep stone base, 3 tall. The piston sits at ground level; the quasi-power source is one layer above and behind it.

steps.title: Piston placement
steps.explanation: Place a regular piston at (1,1,0) facing east. This piston will be quasi-powered — it sees power diagonally from above but won't extend until a block update occurs near it.

steps.title: Quasi-power source
steps.explanation: Place a solid block at (1,2,0) directly above the piston. Place a redstone torch on the SIDE (south face) of that block at (1,2,0) — this powers the block at y=2 which 'quasi-powers' the piston at y=1 below. In Minecraft, pistons check for power one block above their position as well.

steps.title: Observer output
steps.explanation: Place an observer at (2,1,0) facing west (observing the piston's face). When any block update occurs adjacent to the quasi-powered piston, it briefly extends — the observer detects the state change and emits a pulse east. A lamp at (2,0,-1) shows the pulse. Place or break a block near (0,1,0) to trigger it.
